text: Iron is essential to athletic performance due to its roles in oxygen transport and energy production.
Athletes commonly present with iron deficiency, though the severity and its direct effect on performance remain debated.
Exercise-induced iron losses occur through multiple mechanisms including hemolysis, hematuria, sweating, and gastrointestinal bleeding.
The acute-phase inflammatory response to exercise elevates cytokine levels, which stimulate liver production of the hormone hepcidin.
Hepcidin up-regulation negatively impacts iron transport and absorption channels in the body, representing a potential mechanism behind exercise-induced iron deficiency.
Chronic exercise training may produce an inflammatory state analogous to the anemia of chronic disease, contributing to iron deficiency in athletes.
